Added PairwiseComp(). sirius_annot: (optional) SIRIUS/CANOPUS class annotations. Added AddSiriusAnnot(). custom_annot: (optional) Custom compound annotations. Added AddCustomAnnot(). cos.dissim: (optional) Cosine dissimilarity matrix. Added AddChemdist(). m2ds.dissim: (optional) MS2DeepScore dissimilarity matrix. Added AddChemdist(). dreams.dissim: (optional) DreaMS dissimilarity matrix. Added AddChemdist(). can create mmo object like : created, can use mmo object normalization, annotation, statistical analysis, visualization throughout package.
code: mmo <- GetMZmineFeature(   mzmine_dir = "path/to/mzmine_feature.csv",   metadata_dir = "path/to/metadata.csv",   group_col = "treatment" )

title: Intro
text: mendatory input files creating mmo object : MZmine feature table: file generated MZMine (Feature list methods → Export feature list → CSV). See MZMine_documentation Metadata file: CSV file containing sample metadata, following columns. -sample (___.mzML equivalent filename used input MZmine), -mass (sample mass used normalization. just put 1 needed), -least one grouping info column (experimental group sample). Use input ‘group_col’ argument GetMZmineFeature(). Refer Inputs_MZMine details.

title: Intro
text: raw peak area values processed following steps: Replacing missing values (NA) half minimum value feature 1. Normalizing sample mass (provided metadata). Transform data using Log, Mean-centering, Z-score scaling.
code: mmo <- ReplaceZero(mmo, method = 'one') # Replace 0 and NA values by 1 mmo <- MassNormalization(mmo) # Normalize peak area by sample mass in metadata mmo <- MeancenterNormalization(mmo) # Add mean-centered area mmo <- LogNormalization(mmo) # Add log-transformed area mmo <- ZNormalization(mmo) # Add Zscore

title: Treatment-based_study_tutorial
text: tutorial, demonstrate use eCOMET package analyzing metabolomics data treatment-based study. Treatment-based studies involve comparing metabolite profiles different treatment groups (e.g., control vs treated). tutorial covers data preprocessing, normalization, statistical analysis, visualization techniques commonly used treatment-based metabolomics studies. tutorial files metabolomics analysis files Arabidopsis thaliana (Col-0) attacked two different herbivores (Spodoptera litura; sl, Lipaphis erysimi; le). Eight replicates sampled analyzed group. See metadata.csv details. Make sure downloaded tutorial files set working directory downloaded folder.
code: setwd('/path/to/eCOMET/tutorial/tutorial_1_treatment_based')  demo_feature <- "raw_data/feature_table_demo.csv" demo_metadata <- "raw_data/metadata_demo.csv" demo_sirius_formula <- "raw_data/canopus_formula_summary.tsv" demo_sirius_structure <- "raw_data/structure_identifications.tsv" demo_dreams <- "raw_data/dreams_sim_demo.csv" gls_db <- "raw_data/custom_DB_glucosinolates.csv"

title: Treatment-based_study_tutorial
text: Following steps performed create mmo object add various normalizations annotations. Inspect structure ‘mmo’ object using ‘summary(mmo)’’ step see object updated.
code: # Initialize eCOMET object mmo <- GetMZmineFeature(mzmine_dir=demo_feature, metadata_dir = demo_metadata, group_col = 'group') # Add normalizations mmo <- ReplaceZero(mmo, method = 'one') # Replace 0 and NA values by 1 mmo <- MassNormalization(mmo) # Normalize peak area by sample mass in metadata mmo <- MeancenterNormalization(mmo) # Add mean-centered area mmo <- LogNormalization(mmo) # Add log-transformed area mmo <- ZNormalization(mmo) # Add Zscore  # Add SIRIUS annotation mmo <- AddSiriusAnnot(mmo, canopus_structuredir = demo_sirius_structure, canopus_formuladir = demo_sirius_formula) # Make a vector of flavonoids using SIRIUS annotation FLVs <- mmo$sirius_annot %>% filter(str_detect(mmo$sirius_annot[['ClassyFire#most specific class']], "Flavonoid")) %>% pull(feature)  # Add custom annotation using inhouse glucosinolate library mmo <- AddCustomAnnot(mmo, DB = gls_db, mztol = 5, rttol = 0.2) # Make a vector of glucosinolates using custom annotation GLSs <- mmo$custom_annot %>% filter(lengths(custom_annot) > 0) %>% pull(feature)  # Add Dreams distance mmo <- AddChemDist(mmo, dreams_dir = demo_dreams)

title: Treatment-based_study_tutorial
text: PCA plot commonly used visualize overall distribution groups. Following PERMANOVA test can performed check groups significantly different.
code: # Set colors for groups colors <- c("ctrl" = "grey", "sl1" = "#fdcdac", "le1" = "#b3e2cd") # PCA plot # A plot and permanova output files will be generated in the specified outdir PCAplot(mmo, color = colors, outdir = 'plot/PCA') PCAplot(mmo, color = colors, outdir = 'plot/PCA_log', label = FALSE, normalization = 'Log') # Data normalization option PCAplot(mmo, color = colors, outdir = 'plot/PCA_FLV', label = FALSE, filter_feature = TRUE, feature_list = FLVs) # Feature filtering option

title: Treatment-based_study_tutorial
code: # Plot PLS-DA PLSDAplot(mmo, color = colors, outdir = 'plot/PLSDA.pdf') PLSDAplot(mmo, color = colors, outdir = 'plot/PLSDA_meancentered.pdf', normalization = 'Meancentered') # Data normalization option

title: Treatment-based_study_tutorial
text: ‘PairwiseComp()’ function performs pairwise comparison two groups using t-test fold change. results stored mmo$pairwise_comp. ‘GetDAMs()’ function extracts DAMs based user-defined cutoffs p-value fold change. , compare herbivore treatment group (sl1 le1) control group (ctrl). Log2(1.5) = 0.5849625 used fold change cutoff 0.1 p-value cutoff extract DAMs
code: # Run pairwise comparison mmo <- PairwiseComp(mmo, group1 = 'ctrl', group2 = 'sl1') mmo <- PairwiseComp(mmo, group1 = 'ctrl', group2 = 'le1') # Extract DAMs DAMs <- GetDAMs(mmo, fc_cutoff = 0.5849625, pval_cutoff = 0.1) # log2(1.5) = 0.5849625 DAMs_up <- DAMs$DAMs_up DAMs_down <- DAMs$DAMs_down head(DAMs_up)

title: Treatment-based_study_tutorial
text: volcano plot can used visualize overall distribution features based fold change p-value.
code: # Volcano plot VolcanoPlot(mmo, comp = 'ctrl_vs_sl1', outdir = 'plot/Volcano_ctrl_vs_sl1.pdf') VolcanoPlot(mmo, comp = 'ctrl_vs_le1', outdir = 'plot/Volcano_ctrl_vs_le1.pdf', topk = 0) # Remove label by setting topk = 0

title: Treatment-based_study_tutorial
text: Venn diagram upset plot can used visualize overlap DAMs different comparisons.
code: # Define input list VennInput <- list(   sl1.up = DAMs_up$ctrl_vs_sl1.up,   le1.up = DAMs_up$ctrl_vs_le1.up ) # Venn diagram require(ggvenn) library(ggvenn) ggvenn(VennInput, stroke_size = 0.5, set_name_size = 4, show_percentage = FALSE) +   theme(legend.position = "none")  ggsave("plot/Venn_Upreg.pdf", height = 5, width = 5) # UpSet plot require(UpSetR) library(UpSetR) pdf("plot/Upset_Upreg.pdf", 7, 5) upset(fromList(VennInput), nsets=10, nintersects=20,order.by='freq', mainbar.y.label='Features in Set', line.size=1, point.size=4, shade.color='white', text.scale=1, show.numbers=FALSE) dev.off()

title: Treatment-based_study_tutorial
text: visualize relative abundance features across samples, heatmap can generated. features can either clusterd using hierarchical clustering chemical distances (e.g., Dreams distance), following idea Qemistree (Tripathi et al., 2021, Nat Chem. Biol.). input matrix heatmap can either fold change treatment control mean normalized values across samples. Various normalizations can used (None, Log, Meancentered, Z). Groups features can filtered setting ‘filter_group’ ‘filter_feature’ TRUE providing list groups features. input data heatmap generated ‘GenerateHeatmapInputs()’ function, heatmap plotted using ‘pheatmap’ package.
code: library(pheatmap) # Generate input for heatmap # distance is one of the chemical distance (cosine, m2ds, and dreams) for clustering rows # The values can be either fold_change or mean (use option 'summarize') heatmap_inputs <- GenerateHeatmapInputs(   mmo, summarize = 'fold_change', control_group = 'ctrl',    normalization = 'None', distance = 'dreams' ) # This generates fold change matrix between each treatment and control  # The resulting list contains FC_matrix, dist_matrix, row_label, and heatmap_data # A heatmap can be generated using pheatmap pdf("plot/Heatmap_FC_dreams.pdf", width = 10, height = 10) pheatmap(mat = heatmap_inputs$FC_matrix,       clustering_distance_rows = heatmap_inputs$dist_matrix,        clustering_method = "average",       cellwidth = 100,      cellheight = 0.3,      treeheight_row = 100,      fontsize_row = 3,      fontsize_col = 15,      scale = 'none' ) dev.off()  # Either, you can visualize mean normalized values across samples heatmap_inputs <- GenerateHeatmapInputs(   mmo, summarize = 'mean', normalization = 'Z', distance = 'dreams' )  # 'clustering_distance_rows' option make the dendrogram follows chemical distances of features.  #  -Delete this option to visualize the heatmap following cannonical clustering pdf("plot/Heatmap_Mean_Z_clustering.pdf", width = 10, height = 10) pheatmap(mat = heatmap_inputs$FC_matrix,       #clustering_distance_rows = heatmap_inputs$dist_matrix,  # Delete this option to visualize the heatmap following cannonical clustering       clustering_method = "average", #UPGMA      cellwidth = 100,      cellheight = 0.3,      treeheight_row = 100,      fontsize_row = 3,      fontsize_col = 15,      scale = 'none' ) dev.off()  # Feature filtering option # Visualize only glucosinolates # As glucosinolate annotations are stored in mmo$custom_annot (by AddCustomAnnot() function), # the compound names are stored in heatmap_inputs$row_label and can be used as row names heatmap_inputs_GLS <- GenerateHeatmapInputs(   mmo, summarize = 'mean', normalization = 'Z', distance = 'dreams',   filter_feature = TRUE, feature_list = GLSs )  pdf("plot/Heatmap_Mean_Z_GLS.pdf", width = 10, height = 10) pheatmap(mat = heatmap_inputs_GLS$FC_matrix,       #clustering_distance_rows = heatmap_inputs_GLS$dist_matrix,  # Delete this option to visualize the heatmap following cannonical clustering       clustering_method = "average", #UPGMA      cellwidth = 100,      cellheight = 8,      treeheight_row = 100,      fontsize_row = 8,      fontsize_col = 15,      scale = 'none',      annotation_names_row = TRUE,      labels_row = heatmap_inputs_GLS$row_label ) dev.off()

title: Treatment-based_study_tutorial
text: identify overepresented chemical classes set features interest (e.g., DAMs), CANOPUS class enrichment analysis can performed following idea ORA.
code: # For a single set of features, a detailed enrichment plot can be generated # There are two plotting styles available CanopusListEnrichmentPlot(mmo, DAMs_up$ctrl_vs_sl1.up, pthr = 0.1, outdir = 'plot/sl1_up_enrichment.pdf', height = 6, width = 6) CanopusListEnrichmentPlot_2(mmo, DAMs_up$ctrl_vs_sl1.up, pthr = 0.1, outdir = 'plot/sl1_up_enrichment_2.pdf', topn = 10, height = 6, width = 6)  # For a list of sets features, a summary enrichment plot can be generated # The summary enrichment plot can be generated for either a single level of CANOPUS classification (8.2.1) or for all levels (8.2.2) # For a single level of CANOPUS classification term_levels <- c('NPC_class', 'NPC_superclass', 'NPC_pathway', 'ClassyFire_most_specific', 'ClassyFire_level5', 'ClassyFire_subclass', 'ClassyFire_class', 'ClassyFire_superclass') CanopusLevelEnrichmentPlot(mmo, DAMs_up, term_level = 'NPC_class', pthr = 0.1, prefix = 'plot/DAMs_up_NPC_class')   # For all levels of CANOPUS classification # All levels, or only NPC or ClassyFire CanopusAllLevelEnrichmentPlot(mmo, DAMs_up, term_level = 'NPC', pthr = 0.1, prefix = 'plot/DAMs_up_all_terms', width = 8, height = 12) CanopusAllLevelEnrichmentPlot(mmo, DAMs_up, term_level = 'ClassyFire', pthr = 0.1, prefix = 'plot/DAMs_up_all_terms', width = 8, height = 12)

what: A Tool for Mass Spectrometry–Based Ecological Metabolomics
title: A Tool for Mass Spectrometry–Based Ecological Metabolomics
text: eCOMET R package processing analyzing mass spectrometry–based metabolomics data ecological evolutionary contexts. establishes standardized pipeline integrates MS1 feature–abundance tables MS2 spectral similarity generate common data products, including principal component analyses (PCA), chemical dendrograms tips represent compounds, principal coordinates analyses (PCoA), differential accumulation analyses. Unlike existing metabolomics toolkits focus primarily biomedical cheminformatics applications, ecomet designed explicitly ecometabolomics. emphasizes workflows link metabolomic variation ecological data, making easier move raw mass spectrometry files reproducible, comparative analyses plant chemical diversity.

title: A Tool for Mass Spectrometry–Based Ecological Metabolomics
text: Import normalize MS1 feature–abundance tables Integrate MS2 spectral similarity chemical diversity analysis Perform differential accumulation analysis find DAMs (Differentially accumulated metabolites) Visualize results PCA, PLS-DA, PCoA, NMDS, volcano plots, heatmaps, Quantify chemical diversity using alpha beta diversity metrics, utilizing spectral similarity based modified cosine score, MS2DeepScore, DreaMS Chemical class enrichment analysis using CANOPUS annotation Screening molecular features related sample metadata (e.g., temperature, height, herbivory)
